name: rig
description: rig — a quality-gated AI workbench for Claude Code. Give /rig:go (the main entry) a natural-language task: it classifies the task, runs it in an isolated git worktree, and checks the result against an acceptance gate — the diff only lands in your working tree on an explicit accept. Track parallel tasks with /rig:go board; compose recipes, personas, and knowledge as reusable bricks. scripts/mcp_server.py exposes the same task/orchestrate operations over a stdlib-only MCP stdio server, so an external agent, CI, or another process can drive rig without a live Claude Code session — opt-in, no new engine, accept/discard safety unchanged. scripts/workbench.py record-commit/record-outcome/trace-commit close the loop from accept-time gate predictions to real production outcomes — link a commit to its task, record ok/incident, and reverse-look-up a commit to its original gate verdict with a revert-plan draft (never auto-applied). codex/skills/rig/SKILL.md, codex/hooks.json, and .codex/agents/security-reviewer.toml wire rig into Codex CLI's own native layer (Skills/Hooks/Subagent TOML) alongside the existing skill-symlink and --provider codex paths.
